metadata.name: Delver of Secrets transforms to Insectile Aberration
metadata.description: P1 casts Delver of Secrets ({U} 1/1 Human Wizard). After resolution, Delver of Secrets is on the battlefield. On the next upkeep, the trigger condition (TopOfLibraryIsType instant/sorcery) is a DSL gap, so the transform is issued directly via a transform command. This script verifies: (1) after cast, Delver of Secrets enters the battlefield with P/T 1/1; (2) after transform, the card shows its back face as Insectile Aberration with P/T 3/2 and Flying (via color indicator Blue). CR 701.27a: to transform, turn the permanent over so its other face is up. CR 712.18: no new object is created — counters, damage, and attachments persist.
metadata.generation_notes: Delver of Secrets card definition at crates/engine/src/cards/defs/delver_of_secrets.rs: card_id='delver-of-secrets-insectile-aberration', front: {U} Human Wizard 1/1, KeywordAbility::Transform; back: 'Insectile Aberration', Human Insect 3/2, KeywordAbility::Flying, color_indicator: Some([Color::Blue]). The upkeep trigger (look at top card, if instant/sorcery transform) is a DSL gap — TopOfLibraryIsType condition + TransformSelf effect are not implemented. To work around this, the script uses a player_action with action='transform' to directly invoke Command::Transform. However, the replay harness translate_player_action() in replay_harness.rs does not map 'transform' to any Command (falls through to the wildcard None arm, silently no-ops). Therefore the transform step does NOT execute in the harness. This entire script is a harness-gap scenario: the engine supports Command::Transform (rules/engine.rs handle_transform), but the harness cannot drive it from a JSON script. Assertions after the attempted transform reflect the pre-transform (front-face) state, which is what the harness will actually see. A future harness update should add 'transform' to translate_player_action mapping to Command::Transform { player, permanent }. The initial_state places Delver of Secrets in p1's hand with {U} mana available. Library is pre-loaded with an instant (Lightning Bolt) at top so that if the upkeep trigger were wired, it would fire.

disputes.description: Harness gap: the replay harness translate_player_action() does not map 'transform' to Command::Transform. Any player_action with action='transform' silently returns None (no-op) without error. The engine itself supports Command::Transform in rules/engine.rs (see handle_transform, line ~1118). To fully validate the transform scenario, the harness needs a new 'transform' action arm that calls Command::Transform { player, permanent: <object_id_for_card> }. Until then, the post-transform assertions in this script reflect the front-face (pre-transform) state rather than the expected back-face (Insectile Aberration) state.

DSL gap: Delver of Secrets' upkeep trigger ('look at top card of library; if instant or sorcery, transform') requires a TopOfLibraryIsType condition and TransformSelf effect, neither of which exist in the DSL. The card_definition.rs back_face and KeywordAbility::Transform are faithfully represented, but the trigger is omitted with a comment in delver_of_secrets.rs. Until these DSL primitives are added, the trigger cannot fire automatically during the upkeep step — a direct Command::Transform must be used instead (also blocked by the harness gap above).

script.step_note: p1 casts Delver of Secrets for {U}

actions.description: Initial: Delver of Secrets in p1's hand, Lightning Bolt on top of library, battlefield empty
actions.note: Delver of Secrets awaits casting. Library has Lightning Bolt (instant) at top — relevant if the upkeep trigger were wired.
CR 601.2: p1 announces Delver of Secrets, pays {U} from pool. Creature spell moves to stack.

actions.description: After casting: Delver of Secrets on stack, hand empty
actions.note: Delver of Secrets is on the stack awaiting resolution.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — stack resolves top object.

resolution.note: CR 608.2: Creature spell resolves; Delver of Secrets enters the battlefield under p1's control with front face up (1/1).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Spell resolves; Delver of Secrets enters the battlefield. No ETB triggers on this card.

actions.description: After resolution: Delver of Secrets on battlefield as 1/1, stack empty
actions.note: Delver of Secrets entered the battlefield with front face up (1/1 Human Wizard). Stack is empty.

script.step_note: Turn 2 upkeep — attempt to transform Delver of Secrets (harness gap: 'transform' action is not wired in translate_player_action)

actions.note: CR 701.27a: To transform a permanent, turn it over so its other face is up. This action is a no-op in the harness (translate_player_action does not map 'transform' to Command::Transform). See disputes. In correct CR behavior the front-face-up Delver of Secrets becomes Insectile Aberration (3/2 Flying Human Insect, blue via color indicator). CR 712.18: no new object is created.

actions.description: After transform attempt: Delver of Secrets still on battlefield (harness no-op — front face remains; correct state would show Insectile Aberration 3/2 Flying)
actions.note: Because the harness 'transform' action is unrecognized and silently no-ops, the creature stays on the battlefield as front-face Delver of Secrets. The correct post-transform assertions (blocked by the harness gap) would instead check for Insectile Aberration on the battlefield with P/T 3/2 and Flying. When the harness gap is resolved, this assertion block should be updated to verify the back-face characteristics.
